mission.title: Install and Configure Ratify on Kubernetes
mission.description: Ratify is an Artifact Ratification Framework that verifies artifact security metadata (signatures, SBOMs, vulnerability reports) before allowing deployment. It integrates with OPA Gatekeeper as an external data provider to enforce verification policies at admission time.

steps.title: Prerequisites — install helmfile
steps.description:
Ratify's quick-start uses helmfile to deploy Gatekeeper, Ratify, and constraint templates together. Install helmfile (v0.14+, < v1.0.0):
```bash
# macOS
brew install helmfile

# Linux
curl -fsSL -o helmfile https://github.com/helmfile/helmfile/releases/latest/download/helmfile_linux_amd64_v1 && chmod +x helmfile && sudo mv helmfile /usr/local/bin/
```
Verify: `helmfile version` — must be >= 0.14 and < 1.0.0.

steps.title: Deploy Gatekeeper, Ratify, and constraints
steps.description:
Run the helmfile sync command to deploy all components in one step. This installs OPA Gatekeeper (v3.18) with external data enabled, the Ratify chart, and default constraint templates:
```bash
helmfile sync -f 'git::https://github.com/notaryproject/ratify.git@helmfile.yaml?ref=v1.4.0'
```
This deploys into the `gatekeeper-system` namespace. The Ratify helm chart comes from `https://ratify-project.github.io/ratify`.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that Gatekeeper and Ratify pods are running in gatekeeper-system:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system
```
You should see gatekeeper-controller, gatekeeper-audit, and ratify pods all in `Running` state. Also verify the constraint template exists:
```bash
kubectl get constrainttemplates | grep ratify
```

steps.title: Test with a signed image
steps.description:
Verify that Ratify allows a properly signed image:
```bash
kubectl run demo --image=ghcr.io/deislabs/ratify/notary-image:signed -n default
```
This should succeed — the image has a valid Notary v2 signature.

steps.title: Test with an unsigned image
steps.description:
Verify that Ratify blocks an unsigned image:
```bash
kubectl run demo1 --image=ghcr.io/deislabs/ratify/notary-image:unsigned -n default
```
This should be denied by Gatekeeper with an error message indicating signature verification failed.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have Gatekeeper and Ratify pods running in gatekeeper-system. Signed images are allowed while unsigned images are blocked at admission time. You can customize verification policies by modifying the Ratify Store and Verifier custom resources.

uninstall.title: Remove all components with helmfile
uninstall.description:
Destroy the entire helmfile release to remove Gatekeeper, Ratify, and constraint templates:
```bash
helmfile destroy --skip-charts -f 'git::https://github.com/notaryproject/ratify.git@helmfile.yaml?ref=v1.4.0'
```

uninstall.title: Clean up test pods
uninstall.description:
Remove any test pods created during validation:
```bash
kubectl delete pod demo demo1 -n default --ignore-not-found
```

upgrade.title: Update to a new Ratify version
upgrade.description:
Change the ref tag in the helmfile URL to the new version and re-run sync:
```bash
helmfile sync -f 'git::https://github.com/notaryproject/ratify.git@helmfile.yaml?ref=<new-version>'
```
For example, to upgrade to v1.5.0: `ref=v1.5.0`

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that pods are restarted with the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system
kubectl describe pod -n gatekeeper-system -l app=ratify | grep Image:
```

troubleshooting.title: helmfile command not found
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure helmfile is installed and on your PATH:
```bash
helmfile version
```
Ratify requires helmfile >= v0.14 and < v1.0.0. Install via `brew install helmfile` (macOS) or download from the helmfile GitHub releases page.

troubleshooting.title: Gatekeeper pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check Gatekeeper logs and events:
```bash
kubectl logs -n gatekeeper-system -l control-plane=controller-manager
kubectl get events -n gatekeeper-system --sort-by=.lastTimestamp
```
Common issues: insufficient RBAC, webhook port conflicts, or resource constraints.

troubleshooting.title: Ratify pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check Ratify logs for configuration errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n gatekeeper-system -l app=ratify
kubectl describe pod -n gatekeeper-system -l app=ratify
```
Ensure Gatekeeper is running with `enableExternalData=true` — Ratify depends on Gatekeeper's external data provider feature.

troubleshooting.title: Signed images being blocked
troubleshooting.description:
If valid signed images are rejected, check the Ratify verifier configuration and logs:
```bash
kubectl get verifiers -n gatekeeper-system
kubectl logs -n gatekeeper-system -l app=ratify | tail -50
```
Verify the constraint template and constraint are applied: `kubectl get constraints`

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes v1.30 or higher. helmfile >= v0.14 and < v1.0.0. Helm 3 and kubectl configured to access your cluster. OPA Gatekeeper v3.18+ is deployed automatically by the helmfile.
